message:
fix(parser): correct CTE/subquery column resolution and function arity table

The semantic resolver previously registered CTE references, subqueries,
and table functions with an empty HashSet of columns, which caused
legitimate column references against those sources to fail resolution.
By changing the column set to Option<HashSet<String>>, a None value now
signals "columns unknown" and the resolver optimistically accepts any
column reference — matching SQLite's permissive behavior for CTEs and
subqueries whose output schemas are not yet computed.

Also corrects several function arity entries:
- min/max are variadic (scalar multi-arg form), not fixed at 1
- iif and replace require exactly 3 arguments
- coalesce is variadic, not range(2,3)
- ifnull and nullif are exactly 2 arguments
- substr/substring remains range(2,3) but is now separate from replace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
